Sheet1.QUESTIONS: What is the fee structure?
Sheet1.ANSWER:
Approximate yearly fee:
 • Government quota: around ₹50,000 – ₹65,000 per year
 • Management quota: around ₹75,000 – ₹1,00,000 per year
 • Extra charges may include hostel, exam fees, and transportation.

What are the college rules?
Sheet1.ANSWER:
• Attendance: Minimum 75% attendance is required to attend exams, and students must be punctual for all classes and activities.
 • ID Card & Dress Code: Students must wear their ID card on campus and follow the college dress code.
 • Discipline & Cleanliness: Maintain discipline in classrooms and help keep the campus clean.
 • Mobile Phones: Not allowed during classes, labs, or in the library unless permitted.
 • Anti-Ragging: Ragging is strictly prohibited and punishable by law.
 • Grievances: Problems should be reported to the Class Tutor, HOD, or Grievance Cell.
 • Rules: Students must follow all rules; violations may lead to disciplinary action.

Sheet1.QUESTIONS: Why should students choose this college?
Sheet1.ANSWER: Holy Grace Academy of Engineering strives to develop our students to become innovators and thought leaders by imparting a rigorous training programme that utilizes academic, co-curricular, personality development, and the latest pedagogical and technological skills.Students should choose Holy Grace Academy of Engineering because it provides quality education, experienced faculty, and modern facilities. The college offers well-equipped labs, a good library, placement support, and opportunities for sports and extracurricular activities. It helps students develop both technical skills and career opportunities for the future.

Sheet1.QUESTIONS: What is the procedure for adding CE marks?
Sheet1.ANSWER:
For CE (Continuous Evaluation) marks in APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University:
Teachers conduct internal exams, assignments, and attendance evaluation.
Marks are recorded by the subject teacher.
The college uploads CE marks to the KTU portal.
Students can verify the marks in the portal. After verification, the marks are finalized and submitted to the university.

Sheet1.QUESTIONS: What is the pledge to the nation?
Sheet1.ANSWER:
India is my country. All Indians are my brothers and sisters.
I love my country and I am proud of its rich and varied heritage.
I shall always strive to be worthy of it.
I shall give my parents, teachers and all elders respect and treat everyone with courtesy.
To my country and my people, I pledge my devotion.
In their well-being and prosperity alone lies my happiness.

Sheet1.QUESTIONS: What message does the national anthem give?
